title: dichanthelium portoricense subsp. patulum
intro: Dichanthelium portoricense subsp. patulum, also known as Pat's Poverty Grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the island of Puerto Rico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Dichanthelium portoricense subsp. patulum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory problems. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and salves, and is also dried and used in smudging ceremonies for its purifying properties.
